combat_history.event: ROKS Dokdo deployed to provide command and control support following the sinking of ROKS Cheonan by North Korean torpedo, serving as mobile headquarters for search and rescue operations.
combat_history.significance: First major operational deployment demonstrating the platform's value as a command ship during crisis response

combat_history.event: ROKS Dokdo served as primary command platform and medical facility during the Sewol ferry disaster response, coordinating rescue operations and treating survivors.
combat_history.significance: Highlighted the ship's humanitarian assistance and disaster relief capabilities beyond military operations

combat_history.event: Russian Tu-95 and A-50 aircraft violated South Korean airspace over Dokdo island. ROKS Dokdo was conducting exercises in the area, leading to diplomatic tensions with Russia and Japan.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated the political sensitivity of the platform's operations and its role in territorial sovereignty assertions

combat_history.event: Both Dokdo-class ships provided medical support and quarantine facilities during COVID-19 outbreaks, utilizing their extensive medical facilities.
combat_history.significance: Showcased adaptability of the platform for non-traditional security challenges

modernization.description: Integration with KDX-III destroyer data links and improved radar systems for enhanced fleet coordination.
modernization.impact: Significantly improved situational awareness and ability to coordinate with other ROKN vessels

modernization.description: Enhanced medical facilities on both ships with modern surgical equipment and expanded ICU capacity.
modernization.impact: Improved humanitarian assistance capabilities and medical support for extended deployments

modernization.description: Feasibility study for deck strengthening and modifications to support F-35B Lightning II operations.
modernization.impact: Could transform ships into light aircraft carriers, significantly enhancing air power projection

modernization.description: Installation of advanced electronic warfare systems and improved communications security equipment.
modernization.impact: Enhanced survivability in contested electromagnetic environments

vulnerabilities.description: Limited air defense capabilities with only point defense systems (RAM and Phalanx) and no area air defense missiles
vulnerabilities.context: Vulnerable to coordinated air attacks or anti-ship missile saturation, particularly relevant given North Korean missile capabilities
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Relies on escort vessels and shore-based air cover; F-35B modification could provide limited air defense

vulnerabilities.description: Minimal ASW capabilities with no organic sonar suite or anti-submarine weapons
vulnerabilities.context: North Korea operates approximately 70 submarines, making ASW a critical vulnerability in Korean waters
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Requires dedicated ASW escorts; shallow operating areas somewhat limit submarine threat

vulnerabilities.description: Relatively small crew size may limit damage control capabilities during combat operations
vulnerabilities.context: 330 crew members for a 14,000-ton vessel is lean compared to similar platforms
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Advanced automation systems partially compensate, but remains a limiting factor in sustained combat

vulnerabilities.description: Limited fuel and ammunition storage restricts extended independent operations without replenishment
vulnerabilities.context: Designed for regional operations within range of South Korean bases rather than extended blue-water deployments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Focus on regional operations and alliance partnerships for extended missions

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional patrol and exercise operations, primarily in Yellow Sea and East China Sea with periodic deployments to Southeast Asia
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates with KDX-III destroyers, FFX frigates, and support vessels in amphibious ready groups
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Generally high readiness with 70-80% availability rate; maintenance performed at Busan Naval Base

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Hyuga-class is larger (19,000 tons) with better air defense but limited amphibious capability. Dokdo-class optimized for troop transport and landing operations while Hyuga focuses on ASW and air operations.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Korea vs Japan: Competing amphibious strategies in Northeast Asia

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 075 is significantly larger (40,000 tons) with greater aircraft capacity and well deck for landing craft. Dokdo-class represents a more modest regional capability versus China's blue-water ambitions.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: South Korea's amphibious answer to Chinese naval expansion

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar size and mission but Mistral has larger well deck and better command facilities. Dokdo-class has stronger point defenses and is optimized for high-threat regional environment.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: NATO vs Pacific: Different approaches to amphibious warfare design

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Juan Carlos I is larger with ski-jump for fixed-wing aircraft operations. Dokdo-class is more focused on helicopter operations and has better defensive armament for contested environments.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Regional powers: How middle-tier navies approach amphibious capabilities

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Izumo-class being modified for F-35B operations while Dokdo-class remains helicopter-focused. Izumo lacks amphibious capability but superior in air operations and ASW.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Aircraft carrier race: Japan's F-35B modification vs Korea's amphibious focus
